free_text: -Angular dependence of the spin correlation parameter
Aoonn in Np elastic scattering between 0.8 and 1.1 GeV
Saturne national lab. J.B., Ph.C., M.C.,
J.M.F., C.D.L., M.C.L., J.L.S. and experimental site.
Dapnia. J.B., F.L., A.De l., M.De M.,
F.P.-K. and l.Van R.
P.B., Ph.D., G.G., R.H., R.K., D.R., Ph.S.
and B.V.
J.P.G.
R.B., A.K., E.R. and H.S.
L.S.B., Z.J., B.A.K. and Yu.A.U.
D.L. and H.S.
The Saturne II synchrotron
Polarized neutrons were produced by break-up of
vector-polarized deuterons on a 20 cm thick beryllium
target. The break-up protons are swept into a beam
dump. The neutron beam was defined by a total of 8
meters of collimators. At the polarized target the
beam spot was 20 or 30 mm in diameter. The deuteron
beam of 2-3 x 10**11 deuterons/spill produced
6. x 10**7 neutrons/spill at the PPT.
The ion source produced vertically vector-
polarized deuterons. The spin was flipped every
accelerator spill. The polarization of the neutron
beam was typically p=0.59.
the Saclay polarized frozen-spin proton target,
which is 35 mm thick, 40 mm wide and 49 mm high. The
target material was pentanol, with a typical vertical
proton polarization of 85%. The target polarization
was maintained with a vertical magnetic field
of 0.33 Tesla. The target polarization was inverted
every few hours.
.The neutron beam energy spread was about +/- 20 MeV.
The polarization of the neutrons was determined to
0.59. The polarization was checked by measuring also
the break-up proton spin, using a beam polarimeter.
Candidates for good events were deter-
mined and selected by five different pretriggers. The
events from the PPT and the CH2 targets had different
triggers.
Outgoing particles, protons or neu-
trons, were detected by a two-arm spectrometer, which
comprises single scintillation counters, counter hodo-
scopes, a neutron counter hodoscope with its veto
detector, an analyzing magnet and eight multiwire
proportional chambers, each having three wire planes.
.The neutron counter hodoscope consists of 15 scin-
tillating bars and measures the particle position to
within +/- 25 mm from the time difference of the
signals from the two ends. The neutrons scattered at
backward C.M. angles are converted into charged par-
ticles in a carbon plate. These particles are subseq-
uently detected in scintillation counters and MWPCs.
.The efficiency of the neutron counter hodoscope is
15 - 20%, whereas the efficiency for detecting
neutrons scattered in the backward hemisphere
is about 1.5%.
The neutron beam intensity was monitored using
a scintillation counter array inserted in the beam in
front of the target.
.A polarized frozen-spin proton target, PPT.
.To monitor correctly measurements with opposite target
polarizations, a polyethylene target of 3 - 10 mm
thickness was positioned downstream the PPT, and
viewed by a dedicated scintillation counter.
The errors given are statistical errors
only.
(ERR-1) The uncertainties in the polarization
measurements. The systematic error due to the
normalization on the event statistics with different
signs of beam and target polarization to the same
integrated beam intensity was considerably reduced
using additional CH2 target.
.tables 1 - 9 of Nucl.Phys. A559,511,1993
